title: Talent Programs Manager, Early Careers, EMEA
required_skills: Experience in Learning & Development or Talent Programs
Experience working with multiple stakeholders across regions
Strong analytical skills to interpret HR and business data
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills
High level of organisation, planning, and multitasking ability
High attention to detail, integrity, and adaptability
bonus_skills: Exposure to early careers or graduate development initiatives
Experience with Microsoft Teams, SharePoint, and learning platforms
matched_skills: Multi-stakeholder coordination across regions: HSBC cross-functional C-suite, EMEA/APAC coverage; Hapag-Lloyd global organisation
Analytical skills interpreting programme data: HSBC KPI reporting, sprint review analysis at Hapag-Lloyd
Programme management and operations: HSBC 6-workstream programme coordination; programme status, risk escalation
Communication and stakeholder management: C-suite reporting, cross-functional stakeholder management
Microsoft Office proficiency (Excel, PowerPoint): confirmed across multiple roles
Organisation and planning: hybrid event management at HSBC (300+ attendees)
Programme budget tracking: USD 8M programme budget variance dashboards at HSBC
missing_skills: Learning & Development or Talent Programs domain experience (no HR programme delivery background)
Early careers or graduate development programme experience
Microsoft Teams/SharePoint as programme management platforms (not explicitly mentioned)
Graduate programme participant management or alumni network coordination
core_responsibilities: Manage Voyage Graduate Development Program across EMEA properties
Partner with Early Careers Talent Acquisition during recruitment cycles
Coordinate and manage programme frameworks across EMEA
Track programme analytics and performance insights
Ensure structured escalation processes and stakeholder alignment
recommended_emphasis: Lead with HSBC multi-stakeholder programme coordination and EMEA/APAC coverage as direct match for EMEA cross-regional scope
Highlight hybrid event management for 300+ (HSBC town halls) as evidence of programme delivery and participant coordination capability
Emphasise programme budget tracking (USD 8M) as direct match for budget and P&L management responsibility
Frame Hapag-Lloyd KPI reporting and sprint coordination as evidence of programme analytics and performance tracking skills
summary: Match score 65.0% — moderate match. The candidate's programme coordination, EMEA multi-stakeholder management, and budget tracking experience from HSBC map well to this role's operational requirements. The primary gap is the absence of L&D or talent development domain expertise and hotel/hospitality industry background. The transferable programme management and cross-regional coordination skills make this worth applying for, with a cover letter that clearly bridges PMO governance experience to talent programme coordination.
